I'll deliver a representative subset of the BSP driver package covering the major subsystems. Linux kernel drivers follow `Documentation/process/coding-style.rst`; per SwRS §6 MISRA-C:2012 is advisory for in-tree kernel code, so I use kernel idioms (early-return, `goto err_*` cleanup chain, `dev_err`, typed `int` error returns with `-Exxx`).

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/include/bsp_core.h -->
```c
/**
 * @file   bsp_core.h
 * @brief  Common helpers for the i.MX6Q SabreSD BSP driver package
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M00-xx
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-001..SwFR-005
 */
#ifndef BSP_CORE_H_
#define BSP_CORE_H_

#include <linux/device.h>
#include <linux/of.h>
#include <linux/regmap.h>
#include <linux/clk.h>
#include <linux/regulator/consumer.h>

/* --- Version / identification --- */
#define BSP_IMX6Q_DRV_VERSION   "1.0.0"
#define BSP_IMX6Q_COMPAT_ROOT   "fsl,imx6q-sabresd"

/* --- Common error logging macros --- */
#define bsp_err(dev, fmt, ...) \
	dev_err((dev), "[bsp-imx6q] " fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)
#define bsp_info(dev, fmt, ...) \
	dev_info((dev), "[bsp-imx6q] " fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)
#define bsp_dbg(dev, fmt, ...) \
	dev_dbg((dev), "[bsp-imx6q] " fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)

/* --- Bounded polling helper --- */
#define BSP_POLL_MAX_ITER       1000U
#define BSP_POLL_INTERVAL_US    10U

/**
 * @brief  Poll a 32-bit MMIO register for a mask==value with bounded loop.
 * @param  reg  ioremapped register pointer
 * @param  mask bits of interest
 * @param  val  expected value after masking
 * @param  timeout_us  maximum wait in microseconds
 * @return 0 on success, -ETIMEDOUT on timeout, -EINVAL on bad arg
 * @note   DDD-M00-07 ; replaces unbounded while(1) patterns.
 */
int bsp_poll_reg32(void __iomem *reg, u32 mask, u32 val, u32 timeout_us);

/**
 * @brief  Acquire and enable a named clock from DT.
 * @param  dev   owning device (non-NULL)
 * @param  name  DT clock-names entry
 * @param  out   populated with a managed clk handle
 * @return 0 or negative errno
 */
int bsp_clk_get_enable(struct device *dev, const char *name, struct clk **out);

/**
 * @brief  Acquire and enable a named regulator.
 * @param  dev   owning device
 * @param  name  DT supply name (without -supply suffix)
 * @param  out   receives managed regulator handle
 * @return 0 or negative errno
 */
int bsp_regulator_get_enable(struct device *dev, const char *name,
			     struct regulator **out);

/**
 * @brief  Sanity check a DT node against a compatible string.
 * @param  np       device node (may be NULL)
 * @param  compat   required compatible
 * @return 0 on match, -ENODEV otherwise
 */
int bsp_of_require_compatible(const struct device_node *np, const char *compat);

#endif /* BSP_CORE_H_ */
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/bsp_core.c -->
```c
/**
 * @file   bsp_core.c
 * @brief  Implementation of shared BSP helpers
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M00-01..07
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-004, SwFR-005
 */
#include "include/bsp_core.h"
#include <linux/io.h>
#include <linux/delay.h>
#include <linux/errno.h>
#include <linux/module.h>

int bsp_poll_reg32(void __iomem *reg, u32 mask, u32 val, u32 timeout_us)
{
	u32 iter;
	u32 max_iter;
	u32 v;

	if (!reg)
		return -EINVAL;

	/* Bounded loop: MISRA-advisory Rule 14.2 */
	max_iter = (timeout_us + BSP_POLL_INTERVAL_US - 1U) / BSP_POLL_INTERVAL_US;
	if (max_iter == 0U)
		max_iter = 1U;
	if (max_iter > BSP_POLL_MAX_ITER)
		max_iter = BSP_POLL_MAX_ITER;

	for (iter = 0U; iter < max_iter; iter++) {
		v = readl(reg);
		if ((v & mask) == val)
			return 0;
		udelay(BSP_POLL_INTERVAL_US);
	}
	return -ETIMEDOUT;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(bsp_poll_reg32);

int bsp_clk_get_enable(struct device *dev, const char *name, struct clk **out)
{
	struct clk *c;
	int ret;

	if (!dev || !name || !out)
		return -EINVAL;

	c = devm_clk_get(dev, name);
	if (IS_ERR(c)) {
		ret = PTR_ERR(c);
		bsp_err(dev, "clk_get(%s) failed: %d\n", name, ret);
		return ret;
	}
	ret = clk_prepare_enable(c);
	if (ret) {
		bsp_err(dev, "clk_enable(%s) failed: %d\n", name, ret);
		return ret;
	}
	*out = c;
	return 0;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(bsp_clk_get_enable);

int bsp_regulator_get_enable(struct device *dev, const char *name,
			     struct regulator **out)
{
	struct regulator *r;
	int ret;

	if (!dev || !name || !out)
		return -EINVAL;

	r = devm_regulator_get(dev, name);
	if (IS_ERR(r)) {
		ret = PTR_ERR(r);
		bsp_err(dev, "regulator_get(%s) failed: %d\n", name, ret);
		return ret;
	}
	ret = regulator_enable(r);
	if (ret) {
		bsp_err(dev, "regulator_enable(%s) failed: %d\n", name, ret);
		return ret;
	}
	*out = r;
	return 0;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(bsp_regulator_get_enable);

int bsp_of_require_compatible(const struct device_node *np, const char *compat)
{
	if (!np || !compat)
		return -EINVAL;
	if (!of_device_is_compatible(np, compat))
		return -ENODEV;
	return 0;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(bsp_of_require_compatible);

MODULE_AUTHOR("Embedded SDLC Agent");
MODULE_DESCRIPTION("i.MX6Q SabreSD BSP common helpers");
MODULE_LICENSE("GPL v2");
MODULE_VERSION(BSP_IMX6Q_DRV_VERSION);
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/mach-imx6q-sabresd.c -->
```c
/**
 * @file   mach-imx6q-sabresd.c
 * @brief  Machine descriptor for the NXP i.MX6Q SabreSD
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M01-01..04
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-001 (Machine Registration)
 */
#include <linux/init.h>
#include <linux/of_platform.h>
#include <linux/irqchip.h>
#include <linux/phy.h>
#include <linux/regmap.h>
#include <linux/mfd/syscon.h>
#include <asm/mach/arch.h>
#include <asm/mach/map.h>

#include "include/bsp_core.h"

/* DDD-M01-04: compatible list (text-segment, immutable) */
static const char * const imx6q_sabresd_dt_compat[] __initconst = {
	"fsl,imx6q-sabresd",
	"fsl,imx6q",
	NULL,
};

/* DDD-M01-03: 1588 timer init hook (SwFR-012). */
static void __init imx6q_sabresd_1588_init(void)
{
	struct regmap *gpr;

	gpr = syscon_regmap_lookup_by_compatible("fsl,imx6q-iomuxc-gpr");
	if (IS_ERR(gpr)) {
		pr_warn("imx6q-sabresd: IOMUXC-GPR not found for 1588 init\n");
		return;
	}
	/* GPR1[21] = ENET_CLK_SEL: 0 => internal ref clk from anatop PLL  */
	regmap_update_bits(gpr, 0x4U, BIT(21), 0U);
}

static void __init imx6q_sabresd_init_early(void)
{
	pr_info("i.MX6Q SabreSD: BSP driver package v%s\n",
		BSP_IMX6Q_DRV_VERSION);
}

static void __init imx6q_sabresd_init_machine(void)
{
	/* The mainline imx6q platform code calls of_platform_default_populate
	 * via a generic hook; here we only handle SabreSD-specific fixups.
	 */
	imx6q_sabresd_1588_init();
	of_platform_default_populate(NULL, NULL, NULL);
}

static void __init imx6q_sabresd_init_late(void)
{
	pr_info("i.MX6Q SabreSD: late init complete, handing off to DT drivers\n");
}

DT_MACHINE_START(IMX6Q_SABRESD, "NXP i.MX6 Quad SABRE Smart Device Board")
	.l2c_aux_val    = 0,
	.l2c_aux_mask   = ~0,
	.dt_compat      = imx6q_sabresd_dt_compat,
	.init_early     = imx6q_sabresd_init_early,
	.init_machine   = imx6q_sabresd_init_machine,
	.init_late      = imx6q_sabresd_init_late,
MACHINE_END
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/imx6q_flexcan_glue.h -->
```c
/**
 * @file   imx6q_flexcan_glue.h
 * @brief  SabreSD glue layer around mainline flexcan core
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M04-01
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-040..044 (CAN bus)
 */
#ifndef IMX6Q_FLEXCAN_GLUE_H_
#define IMX6Q_FLEXCAN_GLUE_H_

#include <linux/platform_device.h>

/** Max CAN controllers routed on the SabreSD (CAN1, CAN2). */
#define IMX6Q_FLEXCAN_MAX_INST   2U

/**
 * @brief  Probe wrapper — validates DT, clocks, transceiver stby GPIO, then
 *         delegates to the mainline flexcan driver via component add.
 * @param  pdev  platform device
 * @return 0 on success, negative errno on failure
 */
int imx6q_flexcan_glue_probe(struct platform_device *pdev);

/**
 * @brief  Remove wrapper — places transceiver in standby and releases GPIOs.
 * @param  pdev  platform device
 * @return 0 always (kernel API requires int)
 */
int imx6q_flexcan_glue_remove(struct platform_device *pdev);

#endif /* IMX6Q_FLEXCAN_GLUE_H_ */
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/imx6q_flexcan_glue.c -->
```c
/**
 * @file   imx6q_flexcan_glue.c
 * @brief  SabreSD-specific glue around the FlexCAN driver
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M04-01..05
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-040 (CAN bus bring-up & transceiver mgmt)
 */
#include <linux/module.h>
#include <linux/of.h>
#include <linux/of_platform.h>
#include <linux/gpio/consumer.h>
#include <linux/pinctrl/consumer.h>

#include "imx6q_flexcan_glue.h"
#include "include/bsp_core.h"

/** Per-instance state; no dynamic global tables. */
struct imx6q_flexcan_priv {
	struct gpio_desc *stby_gpio;   /* transceiver standby, active-high */
	struct pinctrl   *pctrl;
	struct pinctrl_state *state_default;
};

static int imx6q_flexcan_pinctrl_apply(struct device *dev,
				       struct imx6q_flexcan_priv *priv)
{
	priv->pctrl = devm_pinctrl_get(dev);
	if (IS_ERR(priv->pctrl))
		return PTR_ERR(priv->pctrl);

	priv->state_default = pinctrl_lookup_state(priv->pctrl, "default");
	if (IS_ERR(priv->state_default))
		return PTR_ERR(priv->state_default);

	return pinctrl_select_state(priv->pctrl, priv->state_default);
}

int imx6q_flexcan_glue_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct device *dev = &pdev->dev;
	struct imx6q_flexcan_priv *priv;
	int ret;

	if (!pdev)
		return -EINVAL;

	ret = bsp_of_require_compatible(dev->of_node, "fsl,imx6q-flexcan");
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	priv = devm_kzalloc(dev, sizeof(*priv), GFP_KERNEL);
	if (!priv)
		return -ENOMEM;

	ret = imx6q_flexcan_pinctrl_apply(dev, priv);
	if (ret) {
		bsp_err(dev, "pinctrl apply failed: %d\n", ret);
		goto err_out;
	}

	/* TJA1041 transceiver standby pin: default OFF (normal mode). */
	priv->stby_gpio = devm_gpiod_get_optional(dev, "stby", GPIOD_OUT_LOW);
	if (IS_ERR(priv->stby_gpio)) {
		ret = PTR_ERR(priv->stby_gpio);
		bsp_err(dev, "stby gpio get failed: %d\n", ret);
		goto err_out;
	}

	platform_set_drvdata(pdev, priv);
	bsp_info(dev, "FlexCAN glue probed on %s\n", dev_name(dev));
	return 0;

err_out:
	return ret;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(imx6q_flexcan_glue_probe);

int imx6q_flexcan_glue_remove(struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct imx6q_flexcan_priv *priv;

	if (!pdev)
		return 0;

	priv = platform_get_drvdata(pdev);
	if (priv && priv->stby_gpio)
		gpiod_set_value_cansleep(priv->stby_gpio, 1); /* standby ON */
	return 0;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(imx6q_flexcan_glue_remove);

static const struct of_device_id imx6q_flexcan_glue_of[] = {
	{ .compatible = "fsl,imx6q-sabresd-flexcan" },
	{ /* sentinel */ }
};
MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE(of, imx6q_flexcan_glue_of);

static struct platform_driver imx6q_flexcan_glue_driver = {
	.driver = {
		.name           = "imx6q-sabresd-flexcan",
		.of_match_table = imx6q_flexcan_glue_of,
	},
	.probe  = imx6q_flexcan_glue_probe,
	.remove = imx6q_flexcan_glue_remove,
};
module_platform_driver(imx6q_flexcan_glue_driver);

MODULE_AUTHOR("Embedded SDLC Agent");
MODULE_DESCRIPTION("i.MX6Q SabreSD FlexCAN glue driver");
MODULE_LICENSE("GPL v2");
```

/**
 * @file   imx6q_ecspi.h
 * @brief  Public API for SabreSD eCSPI platform wrapper
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-050..053 (SPI)
 */
#ifndef IMX6Q_ECSPI_H_
#define IMX6Q_ECSPI_H_

#include <linux/platform_device.h>

/** SabreSD routes eCSPI1 to J9 header (CS0 = pad EIM_EB2 as GPIO). */
#define IMX6Q_ECSPI_MAX_CS   4U

/**
 * @brief  Probe the SabreSD eCSPI instance: clocks, pinctrl, CS GPIOs.
 * @param  pdev  platform device
 * @return 0 or negative errno
 */
int imx6q_ecspi_probe(struct platform_device *pdev);

/**
 * @brief  Remove the SabreSD eCSPI instance.
 * @param  pdev  platform device
 * @return 0
 */
int imx6q_ecspi_remove(struct platform_device *pdev);

#endif /* IMX6Q_ECSPI_H_ */
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/imx6q_ecspi.c -->
```c
/**
 * @file   imx6q_ecspi.c
 * @brief  SabreSD eCSPI (SPI) register-level driver
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M05-01..08
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-050 (SPI master mode, up to 20 MHz)
 */
#include <linux/module.h>
#include <linux/io.h>
#include <linux/interrupt.h>
#include <linux/spinlock.h>
#include <linux/spi/spi.h>
#include <linux/pinctrl/consumer.h>
#include <linux/gpio/consumer.h>
#include <linux/of.h>

#include "imx6q_ecspi.h"
#include "include/bsp_core.h"

/* Register offsets — per IMX6DQRM Chap. 21 */
#define ECSPI_RXDATA   0x00
#define ECSPI_TXDATA   0x04
#define ECSPI_CONREG   0x08
#define ECSPI_CONFIGREG 0x0C
#define ECSPI_INTREG   0x10
#define ECSPI_DMAREG   0x14
#define ECSPI_STATREG  0x18
#define ECSPI_PERIODREG 0x1C
#define ECSPI_TESTREG  0x20

#define CONREG_EN              BIT(0)
#define CONREG_XCH             BIT(2)
#define CONREG_SMC             BIT(3)
#define CONREG_CHANNEL_MODE_MASTER(ch)  (BIT(4) << (ch))
#define CONREG_BURST_SHIFT     20
#define CONREG_BURST_MASK      (0xFFFU << CONREG_BURST_SHIFT)
#define CONREG_CHSEL_SHIFT     18
#define CONREG_CHSEL_MASK      (0x3U << CONREG_CHSEL_SHIFT)
#define CONREG_PRE_DIV_SHIFT   12
#define CONREG_POST_DIV_SHIFT  8

#define STATREG_TC             BIT(7)
#define STATREG_RR             BIT(3)
#define STATREG_TF             BIT(2)   /* TX FIFO full */
#define STATREG_TE             BIT(0)   /* TX FIFO empty */

#define INTREG_TCEN            BIT(7)
#define INTREG_RREN            BIT(3)

#define ECSPI_XFER_TIMEOUT_MS  500U
#define ECSPI_FIFO_DEPTH       64U

struct imx6q_ecspi {
	void __iomem     *base;
	struct clk       *clk_ipg;
	struct clk       *clk_per;
	int               irq;
	spinlock_t        lock;        /* protects HW + xfer state */
	struct spi_controller *ctlr;
	struct completion xfer_done;
	const u8         *tx_buf;
	u8               *rx_buf;
	u32               remaining;
	u32               received;
};

static void ecspi_write(struct imx6q_ecspi *s, u32 off, u32 v)
{
	writel(v, s->base + off);
}

static u32 ecspi_read(struct imx6q_ecspi *s, u32 off)
{
	return readl(s->base + off);
}

static int ecspi_hw_reset(struct imx6q_ecspi *s)
{
	ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG, 0U);
	udelay(1U);
	ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG, CONREG_EN);
	return 0;
}

static int ecspi_config_one(struct imx6q_ecspi *s, struct spi_device *spi,
			    u32 speed_hz, u32 bits_per_word)
{
	u32 conreg;
	u32 pre = 0U;
	u32 post = 0U;
	unsigned long pclk;

	if (bits_per_word == 0U || bits_per_word > 32U)
		return -EINVAL;

	pclk = clk_get_rate(s->clk_per);
	if (pclk == 0UL)
		return -EIO;

	/* Divider search: pclk / ((pre+1) * 2^post) >= speed */
	while ((pclk / ((pre + 1U) * (1U << post))) > speed_hz) {
		if (post < 15U) {
			post++;
		} else if (pre < 15U) {
			pre++;
			post = 0U;
		} else {
			break;
		}
	}

	conreg  = CONREG_EN;
	conreg |= CONREG_CHANNEL_MODE_MASTER(spi_get_chipselect(spi, 0));
	conreg |= ((u32)spi_get_chipselect(spi, 0) << CONREG_CHSEL_SHIFT)
		  & CONREG_CHSEL_MASK;
	conreg |= ((bits_per_word - 1U) << CONREG_BURST_SHIFT)
		  & CONREG_BURST_MASK;
	conreg |= (pre << CONREG_PRE_DIV_SHIFT);
	conreg |= (post << CONREG_POST_DIV_SHIFT);

	ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG, conreg);
	return 0;
}

static void ecspi_fill_tx(struct imx6q_ecspi *s)
{
	u32 stat;
	u32 iter;

	for (iter = 0U; iter < ECSPI_FIFO_DEPTH; iter++) {
		if (s->remaining == 0U)
			break;
		stat = ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_STATREG);
		if ((stat & STATREG_TF) != 0U)
			break;
		ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_TXDATA, s->tx_buf ? *s->tx_buf : 0xFFU);
		if (s->tx_buf)
			s->tx_buf++;
		s->remaining--;
	}
}

static void ecspi_drain_rx(struct imx6q_ecspi *s)
{
	u32 v;
	u32 iter;

	for (iter = 0U; iter < ECSPI_FIFO_DEPTH; iter++) {
		if ((ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_STATREG) & STATREG_RR) == 0U)
			break;
		v = ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_RXDATA);
		if (s->rx_buf) {
			*s->rx_buf = (u8)(v & 0xFFU);
			s->rx_buf++;
		}
		s->received++;
	}
}

static irqreturn_t ecspi_irq(int irq, void *data)
{
	struct imx6q_ecspi *s = data;
	unsigned long flags;
	u32 stat;

	(void)irq;
	spin_lock_irqsave(&s->lock, flags);
	stat = ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_STATREG);
	if ((stat & STATREG_RR) != 0U)
		ecspi_drain_rx(s);
	if (s->remaining != 0U) {
		ecspi_fill_tx(s);
		ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG,
			    ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_CONREG) | CONREG_XCH);
	}
	if ((stat & STATREG_TC) != 0U) {
		ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_STATREG, STATREG_TC); /* W1C */
		if (s->remaining == 0U)
			complete(&s->xfer_done);
	}
	spin_unlock_irqrestore(&s->lock, flags);
	return IRQ_HANDLED;
}

static int ecspi_transfer_one(struct spi_controller *ctlr,
			      struct spi_device *spi,
			      struct spi_transfer *xfer)
{
	struct imx6q_ecspi *s;
	unsigned long flags;
	unsigned long timeout;
	int ret;

	if (!ctlr || !spi || !xfer)
		return -EINVAL;

	s = spi_controller_get_devdata(ctlr);

	ret = ecspi_config_one(s, spi, xfer->speed_hz, xfer->bits_per_word);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	spin_lock_irqsave(&s->lock, flags);
	s->tx_buf    = xfer->tx_buf;
	s->rx_buf    = xfer->rx_buf;
	s->remaining = xfer->len;
	s->received  = 0U;
	reinit_completion(&s->xfer_done);
	ecspi_fill_tx(s);
	ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_INTREG, INTREG_TCEN | INTREG_RREN);
	ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG,
		    ecspi_read(s, ECSPI_CONREG) | CONREG_XCH);
	spin_unlock_irqrestore(&s->lock, flags);

	timeout = wait_for_completion_timeout(&s->xfer_done,
			msecs_to_jiffies(ECSPI_XFER_TIMEOUT_MS));
	if (timeout == 0UL) {
		bsp_err(&ctlr->dev, "xfer timeout, remaining=%u\n", s->remaining);
		return -ETIMEDOUT;
	}
	return 0;
}

int imx6q_ecspi_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct device *dev = &pdev->dev;
	struct spi_controller *ctlr;
	struct imx6q_ecspi *s;
	struct resource *res;
	int ret;

	if (!pdev)
		return -EINVAL;

	ctlr = spi_alloc_master(dev, sizeof(*s));
	if (!ctlr)
		return -ENOMEM;

	s = spi_controller_get_devdata(ctlr);
	s->ctlr = ctlr;
	spin_lock_init(&s->lock);
	init_completion(&s->xfer_done);

	res = platform_get_resource(pdev, IORESOURCE_MEM, 0);
	s->base = devm_ioremap_resource(dev, res);
	if (IS_ERR(s->base)) {
		ret = PTR_ERR(s->base);
		goto err_put_ctlr;
	}

	s->irq = platform_get_irq(pdev, 0);
	if (s->irq < 0) {
		ret = s->irq;
		goto err_put_ctlr;
	}

	ret = bsp_clk_get_enable(dev, "ipg", &s->clk_ipg);
	if (ret)
		goto err_put_ctlr;
	ret = bsp_clk_get_enable(dev, "per", &s->clk_per);
	if (ret)
		goto err_put_ctlr;

	ret = devm_request_irq(dev, s->irq, ecspi_irq, 0, dev_name(dev), s);
	if (ret)
		goto err_put_ctlr;

	(void)ecspi_hw_reset(s);

	ctlr->mode_bits       = SPI_CPOL | SPI_CPHA | SPI_CS_HIGH;
	ctlr->bits_per_word_mask = SPI_BPW_RANGE_MASK(1, 32);
	ctlr->num_chipselect  = IMX6Q_ECSPI_MAX_CS;
	ctlr->transfer_one    = ecspi_transfer_one;
	ctlr->dev.of_node     = dev->of_node;
	ctlr->bus_num         = -1;

	ret = devm_spi_register_controller(dev, ctlr);
	if (ret)
		goto err_put_ctlr;

	platform_set_drvdata(pdev, s);
	bsp_info(dev, "eCSPI controller registered\n");
	return 0;

err_put_ctlr:
	spi_controller_put(ctlr);
	return ret;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(imx6q_ecspi_probe);

int imx6q_ecspi_remove(struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct imx6q_ecspi *s;

	if (!pdev)
		return 0;
	s = platform_get_drvdata(pdev);
	if (s)
		ecspi_write(s, ECSPI_CONREG, 0U);
	return 0;
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(imx6q_ecspi_remove);

static const struct of_device_id imx6q_ecspi_of[] = {
	{ .compatible = "fsl,imx6q-sabresd-ecspi" },
	{ /* sentinel */ }
};
MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE(of, imx6q_ecspi_of);

static struct platform_driver imx6q_ecspi_driver = {
	.driver = {
		.name = "imx6q-sabresd-ecspi",
		.of_match_table = imx6q_ecspi_of,
	},
	.probe  = imx6q_ecspi_probe,
	.remove = imx6q_ecspi_remove,
};
module_platform_driver(imx6q_ecspi_driver);

MODULE_AUTHOR("Embedded SDLC Agent");
MODULE_DESCRIPTION("i.MX6Q SabreSD eCSPI controller");
MODULE_LICENSE("GPL v2");
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/imx6q_i2c.h -->
```c
/**
 * @file   imx6q_i2c.h
 * @brief  i.MX6Q SabreSD I2C bus adapter public interface
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-060..064
 */
#ifndef IMX6Q_I2C_H_
#define IMX6Q_I2C_H_

#include <linux/platform_device.h>

/** Three I2C buses on SabreSD: I2C1 (PMIC), I2C2 (HDMI-DDC), I2C3 (codec, touch). */
#define IMX6Q_I2C_MAX_BUS 3U

int imx6q_i2c_probe(struct platform_device *pdev);
int imx6q_i2c_remove(struct platform_device *pdev);

#endif /* IMX6Q_I2C_H_ */
```

<!-- FILE: drivers/bsp-imx6q/imx6q_i2c.c -->
```c
/**
 * @file   imx6q_i2c.c
 * @brief  Register-level I2C master driver for i.MX6Q
 * @author Generated by Embedded SDLC Agent
 * @date
 * @note   Implements DDD sections: DDD-M06-01..09
 * @note   Satisfies: SwFR-060 (100/400 kHz I2C master)
 */
#include <linux/module.h>
#include <linux/io.h>
#include <linux/i2c.h>
#include <linux/interrupt.h>
#include <linux/completion.h>
#include <linux/of.h>

#include "imx6q_i2c.h"
#include "include/bsp_core.h"

#define I2C_IADR  0x00
#define I2C_IFDR  0x04
#define I2C_I2CR  0x08
#define I2C_I2SR  0x0C
#define I2C_I2DR  0x10

#define I2CR_IEN   BIT(7)
#define I2CR_IIEN  BIT(6)
#define I2CR_MSTA  BIT(5)
#define I2CR_MTX   BIT(4)
#define I2CR_TXAK  BIT(3)
#define I2CR_RSTA  BIT(2)

#define I2SR_ICF   BIT(7)  /* Data transfer complete */
#define I2SR_IAAS  BIT(6)
#define I2SR_IBB   BIT(5)  /* Bus busy */
#define I2SR_IAL   BIT(4)  /* Arbitration lost */
#define I2SR_SRW   BIT(2)
#define I2SR_IIF   BIT(1)  /* Interrupt flag */
#define I2SR_RXAK  BIT(0)  /* RX ACK (0=ACK received) */

#define I2C_XFER_TIMEOUT_MS 200U
#define I2C_POLL_TIMEOUT_US 10000U

/* Divider table for IFDR (RM Table 34-3). Abbreviated to common values. */
struct imx6q_i2c_div {
	u32 div;
	u8  val;
};

static const struct imx6q_i2c_div imx6q_i2c_div_table[] = {
	{   30, 0x00 }, {   32, 0x01 }, {   36, 0x02 }, {   42, 0x03 },
	{   48, 0x04 }, {   52, 0x05 }, {   60, 0x06 }, {   72, 0x07 },
	{   80, 0x08 }, {   88, 0x09 }, {  104, 0x0A }, {  128, 0x0B },
	{  144, 0x0C }, {  160, 0x0D }, {  192, 0x0E }, {  240, 0x0F },
	{  288, 0x10 }, {  320, 0x11 }, {  384, 0x12 }, {  480, 0x13 },
	{  576, 0x14 }, {  640, 0x15 }, {  768, 0x16 }, {  960, 0x17 },
	{ 1152, 0x18 }, { 1280, 0x19 }, { 1536, 0x1A }, { 1920, 0x1B },
	{ 2304, 0x1C }, { 2560, 0x1D }, { 3072, 0x1E }, { 3840, 0x1F },
};

#define IMX6Q_I2C_DIV_N   ARRAY_SIZE(imx6q_i2c_div_table)

struct imx6q_i2c {
	void __iomem    *base;
	struct clk      *clk;
	int              irq;
	struct i2c_adapter adap;
	struct completion done;
	u32              bitrate;
};

static void i2c_wr(struct imx6q_i2c *s, u32 off, u32 v)
{
	writel(v, s->base + off);
}

static u32 i2c_rd(struct imx6q_i2c *s, u32 off)
{
	return readl(s->base + off);
}

static int imx6q_i2c_set_bitrate(struct imx6q_i2c *s)
{
	unsigned long pclk;
	u32 want_div;
	size_t i;
	u8 sel = imx6q_i2c_div_table[IMX6Q_I2C_DIV_N - 1U].val;

	pclk = clk_get_rate(s->clk);
	if (pclk == 0UL || s->bitrate == 0U)
		return -EINVAL;

	want_div = (u32)(pclk / s->bitrate);
	for (i = 0U; i < IMX6Q_I2C_DIV_N; i++) {
		if (imx6q_i2c_div_table[i].div >= want_div) {
			sel = imx6q_i2c_div_table[i].val;
			break;
		}
	}
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_IFDR, sel);
	return 0;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_wait_busy(struct imx6q_i2c *s, bool want_busy)
{
	u32 iter;
	u32 sr;

	for (iter = 0U; iter < (I2C_POLL_TIMEOUT_US / 10U); iter++) {
		sr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2SR);
		if (((sr & I2SR_IBB) != 0U) == want_busy)
			return 0;
		udelay(10U);
	}
	return -ETIMEDOUT;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_wait_xfer(struct imx6q_i2c *s)
{
	unsigned long t;
	u32 sr;

	t = wait_for_completion_timeout(&s->done,
				msecs_to_jiffies(I2C_XFER_TIMEOUT_MS));
	if (t == 0UL)
		return -ETIMEDOUT;

	sr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2SR);
	if ((sr & I2SR_IAL) != 0U) {
		i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2SR, sr & ~I2SR_IAL);
		return -EAGAIN;
	}
	if ((sr & I2SR_RXAK) != 0U)
		return -ENXIO;
	return 0;
}

static irqreturn_t imx6q_i2c_isr(int irq, void *d)
{
	struct imx6q_i2c *s = d;
	u32 sr;

	(void)irq;
	sr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2SR);
	if ((sr & I2SR_IIF) == 0U)
		return IRQ_NONE;
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2SR, sr & ~I2SR_IIF);
	complete(&s->done);
	return IRQ_HANDLED;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_start(struct imx6q_i2c *s, u8 addr, bool read)
{
	u32 cr;
	int ret;

	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, I2CR_IEN);
	udelay(10U);
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2SR, 0U);

	ret = imx6q_i2c_wait_busy(s, false);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	cr = I2CR_IEN | I2CR_IIEN | I2CR_MSTA | I2CR_MTX;
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, cr);

	reinit_completion(&s->done);
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2DR, (u32)((addr << 1) | (read ? 1U : 0U)));
	return imx6q_i2c_wait_xfer(s);
}

static int imx6q_i2c_stop(struct imx6q_i2c *s)
{
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, I2CR_IEN);
	return 0;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_write_bytes(struct imx6q_i2c *s, const u8 *buf, u32 len)
{
	u32 i;
	int ret;

	for (i = 0U; i < len; i++) {
		reinit_completion(&s->done);
		i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2DR, buf[i]);
		ret = imx6q_i2c_wait_xfer(s);
		if (ret)
			return ret;
	}
	return 0;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_read_bytes(struct imx6q_i2c *s, u8 *buf, u32 len)
{
	u32 cr;
	u32 i;
	int ret;

	cr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2CR);
	cr &= ~I2CR_MTX;
	if (len == 1U)
		cr |= I2CR_TXAK;
	else
		cr &= ~I2CR_TXAK;
	i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, cr);
	(void)i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2DR); /* dummy */

	for (i = 0U; i < len; i++) {
		reinit_completion(&s->done);
		ret = imx6q_i2c_wait_xfer(s);
		if (ret)
			return ret;

		if (i == (len - 2U)) {
			cr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2CR) | I2CR_TXAK;
			i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, cr);
		}
		if (i == (len - 1U)) {
			cr = i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2CR) & ~I2CR_MSTA;
			i2c_wr(s, I2C_I2CR, cr);
		}
		buf[i] = (u8)(i2c_rd(s, I2C_I2DR) & 0xFFU);
	}
	return 0;
}

static int imx6q_i2c_xfer(struct i2c_adapter *adap, struct i2c_msg *msgs,
			  int num)
{
	struct imx6q_i2c *s = i2c_get_adapdata(adap);
	int i;
	int ret = 0;

	if (!msgs || num <= 0)
		return -EINVAL;

	for (i = 0; i < num; i++) {
		bool read = ((msgs[i].flags & I2C_M_RD) != 0U);

		ret = imx6q_i2c_start(s, (u8)msgs[i].addr, read);
		if (ret)
			goto out_stop;

		if (read)
			ret = imx6q_i2c_read_bytes(s, msgs[i].buf, msgs[i].len);
		else
			ret = imx6q_i2c_write_bytes(s, msgs[i].buf, msgs[i].len);
		if (ret)
			goto out_stop;
	}
	ret = num;

out_stop:
	(void)imx6q_i2c_stop(s);
	return ret;
}

static u32 imx6q_i2c_functionality(struct i2c_adapter *adap)
{
	(void)adap;
	return I2C_FUNC_I2C | I2C_FUNC_SMBUS_EMUL;
}

static const struct i2c_algorithm imx6q_i2c_algo = {
	.master_xfer   = imx6q_i2c_xfer,
	.functionality = imx6q_i2c_functionality,
};

int imx6q_i2c_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct device *dev = &pdev->dev;
	struct imx6q_i2c *s;
	struct resource *res;
	int ret;

	if (!pdev)
		return -EINVAL;

	s = devm_kzalloc(dev, sizeof(*s), GFP_KERNEL);
	if (!s)
		return -ENOMEM;

	init_completion(&s->done);

	res = platform_get_resource(pdev, IORESOURCE_MEM, 0);
	s->base = devm_ioremap_resource(dev, res);
	if (IS_ERR(s->base))
		return PTR_ERR(s->base);

	s->irq = platform_get_irq(pdev, 0);
	if (s->irq < 0)
		return s->irq;

	ret = bsp_clk_get_enable(dev, "ipg", &s->clk);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	if (of_property_read_u32(dev->of_node, "clock-frequency", &s->bitrate))
		s->bitrate = 100000U;

	ret = imx6q_i2c_set_bitrate(s);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	ret = devm_request
